[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2019-06-17
[TITLE]: Toronto Raptors celebration parade marred by violence, transit shutdowns - The Globe and Mail
[METADATA/INTRO]: Enormous crowds filled downtown Toronto on Monday to cheer on their team as the city celebrated its first NBA Championship, and first major championship in more than 20 years – but the festivities were marred by several isolated incidents. Hundreds...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Enormous crowds of Raptors fans packed a route through Toronto’s downtown to see the NBA champions first-hand. Hours later than scheduled, the team arrived at Nathan Phillips Square where they thanked fans for the outpouring of support. 
 But authorities had estimated Sunday that close to two million people would show up to watch the parade. 
 Stephen Doig, a professor at Arizona State University and an expert on crowd-size estimation, looked at aerial photos of the crowds and estimated the number was likely 600,000 at the most. 
 “You are safe to say ‘hundreds of thousands’ came out to see the parade. That’s a fabulous crowd, and a great credit to the Raptors and their fans. But saying 'two million’ is, frankly, ridiculous," he said.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2019
[TITLE]: The 13 best photos of the Raptors championship parade
[METADATA/INTRO]: The 13 best photos of the Raptors championship parade Charles Curtis...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The 13 best photos of the Raptors championship parade 
 June 17, 2019, 2:40 p.m. ET 
 The overhead shots from news helicopters of the Toronto Raptors parade were stunning, showing what looked like the entire city coming out to pay tribute to the 2018-19 NBA champions who took down the Golden State Warriors in six games. 
 Up close, there was so much to take in. There was Fake Kawhi Leonard, and the real one's "Board man gets paid" shirt. There were fantastic signs and Drake, along with Drake's custom 767 "Air Drake" plane flying over. And of course there was Kyle Lowry trying to get Leonard to re-sign with the franchise by getting fans in on the act.
